Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-23T14:34:46Z","updated_at":"2024-11-25T11:11:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

See MWE. `np.union1d` is upcasting an int to a float when only one argument is given.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
assert np.union1d((1,), (1,)).dtype == np.int64
assert np.union1d((), (1,)).dtype == np.float64
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### Python and NumPy Versions:

Both on:
```
2.1.3
3.11.4 (main, Jul  5 2023, 08:40:20) [Clang 14.0.6 ]
```

```
1.26.4
3.11.4 (main, Jul  5 2023, 08:40:20) [Clang 14.0.6 ]
```

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

### Context for the issue:

_No response_","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27822/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27822/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2497711355","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27822#issuecomment-2497711355","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27822","id":2497711355,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86U4Az7","user":{"login":"LogSunny","id":73735008,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjczNzM1MDA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/73735008?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny","html_url":"https://github.com/LogSunny","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-25T11:11:00Z","updated_at":"2024-11-25T11:11:00Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Most likely there is some default conversion happening inside the `np.union1d()` function (e.g. from `tuple` to `array`). 
When you pass an empty object (`tuple`) to `np.array()`, it gets interpreted as an array of unspecified data type. 
By default, NumPy assigns the dtype `float64` to this empty array. This behavior is due to how NumPy handles the default `dtype` when it cannot infer a more specific type from the input data.
You can use `np.array((), dtype=np.int64)` instead of `tuple()`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2497711355/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27324","id":2700782525,"node_id":"I_kwDOAAnGFs6g-qu9","number":27324,"title":"bug in `S('0.5').round()`","user":{"login":"80avin-sw","id":97439243,"node_id":"U_kgDOBc7OCw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/97439243?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw","html_url":"https://github.com/80avin-sw","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/80avin-sw/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":83720928,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4MzcyMDkyOA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/labels/core","name":"core","color":"0000FF","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-28T04:52:33Z","updated_at":"2024-11-29T07:44:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"A bug in round function which makes it deviate from the python's implementation of round and otherwise expected behaviour.

```python
from sympy import S

## sympy's round
S('0.005').round(2)
# 0.0
S('0.5').round()
# 0

## python's round
round(0.005, 2)
# 0.01
round(0.5)
# 1

```

Expected answer is  `S('0.005, 2').round(2) == 0.01` and `S('0.5').round() == 1`, which is what python gives and sympy used to give before the PR https://github.com/sympy/sympy/pull/16608/files
All programming languages ( that I know of ) support this behaviour. I even tried in https://www.geogebra.org/classic and https://www.desmos.com/calculator, they also give same output

This case isn't mentioned in the PR description or conversation, that's why I'm thinking it is an unnoticed bug.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2507252347","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/27324#issuecomment-2507252347","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/27324","id":2507252347,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAnGFs6VcaJ7","user":{"login":"LogSunny","id":73735008,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjczNzM1MDA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/73735008?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny","html_url":"https://github.com/LogSunny","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/LogSunny/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-29T07:44:53Z","updated_at":"2024-11-29T07:44:53Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"The issue you're encountering, when rounding `0.005` to 2 decimal places results in `0.0` instead of 0.01, stems from the rounding rule being applied in the method.

You can found `round` method in `sympy/core/expr.py`. 

In the rounding logic, the method is likely using "round half to even" or "bankers' rounding", which is a standard method of rounding when the number is exactly halfway between two possible rounded values. 

Here is a intro to [Bankers' Rounding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_754#Roundings_to_nearest).

**Banker's rounding** (also known as **round half to even**) is commonly used in many programming languages and libraries because it helps reduce bias in repeated rounding operations and mitigates cumulative errors that can occur over large datasets. (From ChatGPT)

Hope this helps you.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2507252347/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I think if you use `Sympy` as a mathematical tool, you should pay attention to precision. When you must use **Round Half away from zero**  (for example, to meet the requirements of printing or text output), using Python's built-in decimal library may be your better choice., where you can freely control the rounding method.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/2507663311/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
